mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.6% as of 2014-01-15 shows ongoing labor market recovery from post-2008 trough
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal accelerating residential construction activity and household formation, which increases demand for materials, labor, and consumer durables, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 999k units as of 2013-12-15 reflects post-crisis housing recovery
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST acceleration triggers are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at near-zero levels combined with stable inflation signals accommodative monetary policy stance that suppresses real discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing sustained positive returns over 20 trading days when both conditions hold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 234.582 as of 2014-01-15
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above trend indicates robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, signaling strong corporate profitability and capital investment, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.0251 as of 2014-01-15 shows post-recession normalization
regime_hint: cyclical expansion with rising industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration do not exceed baseline by at least 1% (mean difference, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with rising payroll employment signals strengthening consumer purchasing power and demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations over a 5-day horizon as market reprices earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 427.829B and PAYEMS at 137.499M as of 2014-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth exceeding 0.2% monthly signals tight labor market conditions and rising wage pressures, which compress profit margins and increase cost inflation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.499M as of 2014-01-15 reflects moderate post-crisis employment recovery
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following high PAYEMS growth are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals erosion of real purchasing power and triggers expectations of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors discount higher future discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 234.582 as of 2013-12-15 reflects subdued inflation in post-crisis environment
regime_hint: inflation acceleration regime approaching monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following CPI acceleration are not significantly negative (mean return > 1%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly combined with sustained housing starts above 900k units signals broad-based economic expansion and confidence in future demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings forecasts are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2% annualized AND HOUST > 900k units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15965.6B and HOUST at 999k as of 2013-10-15 and 2013-12-15 respectively
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with GDP acceleration and housing recovery
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 65% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
